requirements.title: Package naming match
requirements.requirement: The zip filename and internal top-level folder name shall match.
requirements.rationale: Matching names make package intake, auditing, and handoff less ambiguous.
requirements.acceptance_test: Open the zip and verify the top-level folder name equals the zip basename.

requirements.title: Start-here documentation
requirements.requirement: The package shall include `README_START_HERE.md` at the root.
requirements.rationale: A reviewer or AI agent needs a deterministic first file to read.
requirements.acceptance_test: Confirm the file exists and explains package purpose, usage, and audit commands.

requirements.title: Document control metadata
requirements.requirement: Every markdown document shall include a machine-readable document-control header with document ID, title, version, revision, status, last-updated date, package ID, and normative status.
requirements.rationale: The package itself should demonstrate the documentation discipline it recommends.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/package_structure_audit.py` and verify all markdown files pass the metadata check.

requirements.title: Machine-referenceable requirements
requirements.requirement: Every normative package requirement shall have a stable ID using the `REQ-*` pattern.
requirements.rationale: Requirements must be traceable across acceptance tests, feature inventory, workplans, and audit reports.
requirements.acceptance_test: Confirm this document contains no unnumbered normative requirements and that each `REQ-*` has a linked verification item.

requirements.title: Documentation index
requirements.requirement: The package shall include a documentation index listing the major documents and their intended use.
requirements.rationale: Reviewers should be able to navigate the package without hidden chat context.
requirements.acceptance_test: Confirm `docs/000_DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md` exists and lists the package documents.

requirements.title: Feature inventory
requirements.requirement: The package shall include `FEATURE_INVENTORY.md` with stable `FEAT-*` IDs.
requirements.rationale: Feature preservation depends on knowing what the package currently contains.
requirements.acceptance_test: Confirm each listed feature has an ID, description, status, and verification method.

requirements.title: Acceptance test plan
requirements.requirement: The package shall include `ACCEPTANCE_TEST_PLAN.md` with stable `TEST-*` IDs mapped to requirements.
requirements.rationale: A requirement without a test is not enforceable.
requirements.acceptance_test: Confirm every Must requirement in this file has at least one mapped `TEST-*` item.

requirements.title: Structure audit tool
requirements.requirement: The package shall include a runnable structure audit tool under `tools/`.
requirements.rationale: The package should be self-checking and should generate copy/pasteable reports.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/package_structure_audit.py` and confirm the report is written to `reports/package_structure_audit_report.txt`.

requirements.title: Smoke test
requirements.requirement: The package shall include a runnable smoke test under `tests/`.
requirements.rationale: The package should demonstrate the minimum test harness expected of future code packages.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tests/smoke_test.py` and confirm the report is written to `reports/smoke_test_report.txt`.

requirements.title: Generalized wording
requirements.requirement: The deliverable package shall not contain direct personal-name references from the source draft.
requirements.rationale: The package is intended as a reusable consumer-facing seed, not a personal working note.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run the audit tool and confirm the personal-reference scan passes.

requirements.title: Known limitations
requirements.requirement: The package shall include known limitations and make clear that it is an example seed, not a complete production application.
requirements.rationale: The package should not overclaim completeness.
requirements.acceptance_test: Confirm `KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md` exists and includes first-pass limitations.

requirements.title: GitHub-facing repository README
requirements.requirement: The package shall include a root `README.md` suitable for GitHub visitors and shall direct users to the package start-here workflow.
requirements.rationale: Added for REV-002 GitHub practice-space readiness.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/run_all_audits.py` and verify the linked test passes.

requirements.title: Repository hygiene files
requirements.requirement: The package shall include `.gitignore`, `.gitattributes`, and `.editorconfig`.
requirements.rationale: Added for REV-002 GitHub practice-space readiness.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/run_all_audits.py` and verify the linked test passes.

requirements.title: GitHub collaboration files
requirements.requirement: The package shall include `.github/` issue templates, a pull request template, and baseline workflows.
requirements.rationale: Added for REV-002 GitHub practice-space readiness.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/run_all_audits.py` and verify the linked test passes.

requirements.title: Local/CI audit parity
requirements.requirement: The GitHub CI workflow shall call the same all-audits command available to zip-package recipients.
requirements.rationale: Added for REV-002 GitHub practice-space readiness.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/run_all_audits.py` and verify the linked test passes.

requirements.title: Archive/root name match
requirements.requirement: Release packaging shall verify that the zip basename and internal root folder name match.
requirements.rationale: Added for REV-002 GitHub practice-space readiness.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/run_all_audits.py` and verify the linked test passes.

requirements.title: Security policy
requirements.requirement: The package shall include `SECURITY.md` defining reporting and no-secrets expectations.
requirements.rationale: Added for REV-002 GitHub practice-space readiness.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/run_all_audits.py` and verify the linked test passes.

requirements.title: Lightweight secret scan
requirements.requirement: The package shall include a local secret-pattern scan that writes a copy/pasteable report.
requirements.rationale: Added for REV-002 GitHub practice-space readiness.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/run_all_audits.py` and verify the linked test passes.

requirements.title: Safety baseline
requirements.requirement: The package shall include `SAFETY.md` defining safety classes and baseline live-control rules.
requirements.rationale: Added for REV-002 GitHub practice-space readiness.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/run_all_audits.py` and verify the linked test passes.

requirements.title: License decision gate
requirements.requirement: The package shall include an explicit license-decision gate before public reuse is claimed.
requirements.rationale: Added for REV-002 GitHub practice-space readiness.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/run_all_audits.py` and verify the linked test passes.

requirements.title: Practice-space doctrine
requirements.requirement: The package shall document AISOP as a branchable, auditable space of practices.
requirements.rationale: Added for REV-002 GitHub practice-space readiness.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/run_all_audits.py` and verify the linked test passes.

requirements.title: AI agent handoff protocol
requirements.requirement: The package shall include instructions for AI-assisted revision that require file inspection, revision updates, audit execution, and feature preservation.
requirements.rationale: Added for REV-002 GitHub practice-space readiness.
requirements.acceptance_test: Run `python tools/run_all_audits.py` and verify the linked test passes.
